mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in high PAYEMS periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weak demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as construction sector weakness propagates.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in low HOUST periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < -0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 255) combined with low rates (FEDFUNDS < 0.1%) creates stagflation risks, causing S&P 500 declines over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in stagflation scenarios exceed 0.5% or Rank IC > 0.05 in overlapping windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong supply-side capacity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days via improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in high INDPRO periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 60-day decline in GDP (GDPC1) signals recession risk, causing S&P 500 declines over 60 trading days through reduced consumer spending expectations.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in low GDP periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < -0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 14% triggers fiscal stimulus expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days via policy anticipation.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in high UNRATE periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Weak consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) creates conflicting demand signals, causing S&P 500 volatility over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in mixed-demand scenarios exceed 0.5% or Rank IC > 0.05 in overlapping windows.
